title: Rich German Shiitake Mushroom Bowl
description: A nutty, molten flexitarian bowl featuring sautéed shiitake mushrooms, barley, and melt‑in‑the‑mouth Gruyère.
instructions: Heat butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until the butter foams.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and thyme leaves and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sliced shiitake mushrooms and sauté, stirring occasionally, until they begin to brown, about 5 minutes.
Pour in white wine, letting it reduce by half to concentrate flavor, about 3 minutes.
Add vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then cover and cook until the mushrooms are fully tender, about 8 minutes.
Stir in cooked barley or quinoa and grated Gruyère cheese, allowing the cheese to melt into a creamy broth, about 4 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then remove from heat.
Ladle the mixture into a lacquerware bowl, ensuring an even distribution of mushrooms and sauce.
Garnish with freshly chopped parsley and a light drizzle of olive oil before serving.
